source.title: Mesolimbic dopamine signals the value of work.

confidence_rationale: Direct empirical demonstration (microdialysis + fast-scan voltammetry + optogenetics, rats) that minute-by-minute nucleus accumbens dopamine covaries with reward rate and motivational vigor, and that altering dopamine immediately changes willingness to work. This is the closest available measurement of the exact reward-rate -> vigor coupling MECH-320 posits for v_t. Gold-standard methods and venue; small discount for one nuance (a single shared learning+motivation variable) and animal->REE transfer.
failure_signatures: Dopamine conveyed a SINGLE rapidly-evolving decision variable used for BOTH learning and motivation; MECH-320 treats vigor as separable from learning-rate effects, so this unification is a partial tension -- supportive of the vigor coupling but cautioning against assuming a clean architectural split between v_t and learning signals.

The reward-rate/vigor covariation is correlational at the minute scale; causal manipulation showed dopamine changes willingness to work but the average-reward-rate readout itself is inferred, not independently clamped.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Across timescales, nucleus accumbens dopamine release covaries minute-by-minute with reward rate and motivational vigor and second-by-second with a temporally discounted value function; dopamine conveys a single rapidly-evolving decision variable -- the reward available for investment of effort -- used for both learning and motivation, and changing dopamine immediately alters willingness to work.
mapping.ree_translation: The minute-scale reward-rate/vigor covariation is the empirical signature of MECH-320's v_t: a slow EWMA over the realised E3-score stream whose magnitude scales the action-vs-no-op bias. 'Changing dopamine immediately alters willingness to work' is the causal analogue of v_t shifting the additive bias on action trajectories in E3 scoring.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Hamid et al. find one shared variable serving learning AND motivation; MECH-320 isolates the motivational/vigor use and asserts distinctness from learning-rate and from MECH-313 noise. The paper supports the coupling but does not endorse the architectural separation -- the mapping must own that the biology may be less modular than the REE decomposition.
mapping.source_context: Rats; nucleus accumbens dopamine via microdialysis and fast-scan cyclic voltammetry, with optogenetic manipulation, during adaptive decision-making.

confidence_components.notes: Nature Neuroscience, Berke lab, complementary gold-standard neurochemical methods plus causal optogenetics -- very high source quality. Mapping fidelity high (measures the exact reward-rate->vigor coupling) but trimmed by the single-shared-variable finding that sits in mild tension with MECH-320's modularity. Transfer risk the standard rodent-neurochemistry -> REE-scalar gap.
